behavior_summary: U8P_BURNDOUT::look is a simple process that runs when the player 'looks at' or examines the BURNDOUT entity.
When invoked it emits a single short bark: "burned out fire ".
After the bark the process suspends/terminates (inferred from a trailing suspend node), so no further actions, branching, or state changes occur.
No global flags are read or written and there are no external calls in the snippet; behavior is linear and deterministic.
notable_control_flow: Linear: immediate bark followed by suspend — no conditional branches, menus, or loops present.
Entry is event-driven: the process executes on the U8P_BURNDOUT::look/examine event for the object/NPC (process type 0x0208).
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "burned out fire " intentional for concatenation elsewhere, or an artifact?
Does the engine attach any implicit U8P_BURNDOUT::look-response bookkeeping when a U8P_BURNDOUT::look process suspends (e.g., incrementing inspection counters) that this snippet relies on?
Are there any game-wide conventions tied to process type 0x0208 that would alter lifecycle beyond the visible suspend?

main_function.name: U8P_BURNDOUT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive response when the player examines the BURNDOUT entity by playing a single bark and then ending the U8P_BURNDOUT::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs a 'U8P_BURNDOUT::look' or inspect action against this object/NPC.
Classified as a U8P_BURNDOUT::look-type process (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_BURNDOUT::look
function_map.role: Handles the player 'U8P_BURNDOUT::look' (examine) action for the BURNDOUT entity; plays a single bark then suspends.
